---
title: Roadmap
---

Ideas and planned work per submodule. Checked items are done, unchecked are planned.

## General

- [ ] Package scaffolding (`pyproject.toml`, CI, tests)
- [ ] Examples folder with notebooks demonstrating each submodule
- [ ] Docs site (Quarto) with API reference per submodule

## `q.stats` — return-stream statistics

Stateless analytics for periodic return streams (no plotting dependency),
plus an optional bound `q.stats.returns(...)` object for chaining stats and
plots. Consumed by `q.plot` for rendering.

- [x] `q.stats.performance(returns)` — Total Return, CAGR, Volatility, Sharpe, Sortino, Calmar, Max Drawdown, Win Rate
- [x] `q.stats.alpha(returns, benchmark)` / `q.stats.beta(returns, benchmark)`
- [x] `q.stats.rolling_alpha` / `q.stats.rolling_beta` / `q.stats.rolling_sharpe` / `q.stats.rolling_volatility`
- [x] `q.stats.benchmark_stats(returns, benchmark)` — active return, beta, alpha, correlation, tracking error, information ratio
- [x] `q.stats.monthly_returns(returns)` — year-by-month compounded return table
- [x] `q.stats.returns(returns, benchmark=...)` — bound object exposing chained stats (`.alpha()`, `.beta()`, ...) and `.plot(kind=...)`
- [ ] Animated rolling-beta / CAPM regression plot (`q.stats.returns(...).plot("capm")`), auto-detecting bar timestep with an overridable rolling window
- [ ] `q.load_settings("settings.json")` — project-level defaults (rolling window, annualization, theme) that override library defaults without cluttering call sites
- [ ] tsfresh-style automated return-stream feature extraction
- [x] Additional risk/return ratios, gap-analyzed against [quantstats](https://github.com/ranaroussi/quantstats)'s `stats.py`:
  - [x] Smart Sharpe / Smart Sortino (autocorrelation-penalized; `sharpe(smart=True)`, `autocorr_penalty`)
  - [x] Probabilistic Sharpe / Sortino / Adjusted-Sortino Ratio (`probabilistic_ratio`)
  - [x] Adjusted Sortino (÷√2, Schwager; `adjusted_sortino`)
  - [x] Treynor Ratio (`treynor_ratio`)
  - [x] Omega Ratio (`omega`)
  - [x] Gain-to-Pain Ratio (`gain_to_pain_ratio`)
  - [x] Risk-Adjusted Return, CAGR/exposure (`rar`)
  - [x] Skew / Kurtosis (`skew`, `kurtosis`)
  - [x] Ulcer Index / Ulcer Performance Index (`ulcer_index`, `ulcer_performance_index`)
  - [x] Serenity Index (`serenity_index`)
  - [x] Risk of Ruin (`risk_of_ruin`)
  - [x] Value at Risk (VaR) / Conditional VaR (Expected Shortfall) (`value_at_risk`, `conditional_value_at_risk`)
  - [x] Tail Ratio (`tail_ratio`)
  - [x] Payoff/Win-Loss Ratio, Profit Ratio, Profit Factor (`payoff_ratio`, `profit_ratio`, `profit_factor`)
  - [x] CPC Index, Common Sense Ratio (`cpc_index`, `common_sense_ratio`)
  - [x] Outlier Win/Loss Ratio (`outlier_win_ratio`, `outlier_loss_ratio`)
  - [x] Recovery Factor, Risk-Return Ratio (`recovery_factor`, `risk_return_ratio`)
  - [x] Kelly Criterion (`kelly_criterion`)
  - [x] Best/Worst period, Consecutive Wins/Losses, Exposure (time-in-market), Avg Win/Loss/Return (`best`, `worst`, `consecutive_wins`, `consecutive_losses`, `exposure`, `avg_return`, `avg_win`, `avg_loss`)
  - [x] Return distribution analysis by period w/ IQR outlier detection (`distribution`)
  - [x] R² vs benchmark (`r_squared`)
  - [x] `q.stats.compare(returns, benchmark)` — side-by-side strategy-vs-benchmark aggregated table (`compare`)
- [x] `q.stats.drawdown_details(returns)` — per-episode drawdown table (start/valley/end/days/max DD), sorted by depth or duration; prerequisite for `q.plot`'s longest-drawdown highlighting (quantstats `to_drawdown_series` + `drawdown_details`)
- [x] Monte Carlo simulation (`q.stats.montecarlo`) — bootstrap-resampled (with replacement, not quantstats' order-invariant shuffle) return simulation with bust/goal probability and percentile confidence bands, plus an optional stationary block bootstrap (`block_size`) preserving autocorrelation/volatility clustering that i.i.d. resampling destroys, and a `periods` parameter decoupling the simulated horizon from the (long, multi-regime) resampling pool length — avoiding both single-regime sampling bias and the saturated bust/goal probabilities and unreadable terminal-value scale that result from compounding over an unrealistically long horizon (a limitation quantstats' own `_montecarlo.py` shares) (quantstats `_montecarlo.py`)
- [x] Variance Testing (`q.stats.variance_test`) — forward, trade-by-trade simulator that stress-tests a strategy's edge by randomly perturbing its historical win rate (`win_rate_variance`) per simulation and resampling win/loss trade returns accordingly over an `periods`-trade horizon, reporting make-money/ruin probabilities, average profit/drawdown, a profit-to-drawdown (`pnldd_ratio`) ratio, and percentile confidence bands — expressed in compounded returns rather than account-currency dollars, unlike typical dollar-denominated variance-testing tools
- [x] Noise Test (`q.stats.noise_test`) — sensitivity simulator that keeps the exact historical sequence of returns but perturbs each period's return magnitude with multiplicative Gaussian noise (`noise`, scaled to the return series' own volatility) without flipping its sign, asking whether a strategy holds up under different day-to-day noise/volatility rather than a different resampled order (`montecarlo`) or win rate (`variance_test`); reports the same bust/goal probability and percentile-confidence-band shape as `montecarlo`, expressed in compounded returns
- [x] Return-prep utilities: `aggregate_returns` (resample to W/M/Q/Y), `make_portfolio` (combine returns into an equity curve with rebalancing modes), `to_prices`/`rebase` helpers (quantstats `utils.py`)
- [x] Standalone metrics currently only computed inline inside `performance()`/`benchmark_stats()`, gap-analyzed against quantstats' `stats.py`:
  - [x] `to_drawdown_series(returns)` — running drawdown series (also a prerequisite for `drawdown_details`)
  - [x] `max_drawdown(returns)` — standalone Max Drawdown
  - [x] `volatility(returns)` — standalone annualized volatility
  - [x] `calmar(returns)` — standalone Calmar ratio
  - [x] `win_rate(returns)` — standalone win rate
  - [x] `information_ratio(returns, benchmark)` — standalone Information Ratio
- [x] Return-transform & descriptive utilities, gap-analyzed against quantstats' `stats.py`/`utils.py`:
  - [x] `excess_returns(returns, rf)` — public per-period excess-return series (quantstats `to_excess_returns`)
  - [x] `to_log_returns(returns)` — convert simple returns to log returns (quantstats `to_log_returns`)
  - [x] `exponential_volatility(returns)` — EWM-weighted volatility (quantstats `exponential_stdev`)
  - [x] `geometric_mean(returns)` — geometric mean return per period (quantstats `expected_return`/`geometric_mean`/`ghpr`)
  - [x] `outliers(returns)` / `remove_outliers(returns)` — quantile-based outlier filter (distinct from the IQR-based `distribution`)


## `q.plot` — plotting with premade quant-finance configs

Opinionated plotting helpers with sensible defaults for quant research.

- [ ] `q.plot.col(df, "cum_trade_log_return")` — quick single-column plot with clean defaults
- [ ] Multi-column rendering **with wildcards**: `q.plot.col(df, "*_log_ret")` plots all columns matching the pattern (e.g. multiple return fields at once)
- [ ] `q.plot.plot(...)` — enriched plot variant with stats and graphical extras:
  - [ ] Underwater (drawdown) plot
  - [ ] Longest drawdown highlighted, with dates on drawdown periods (needs `q.stats.drawdown_details`; quantstats `plot_longest_drawdowns`)
  - [ ] Annotations of notable historical events (Covid, wars, etc.)
  - [ ] Key metrics overlaid/inset (Sharpe, CAGR, max DD, ...)
  - [ ] Returns distribution / histogram by period, with outliers marked (quantstats `plot_distribution`, `plot_histogram`)
  - [ ] Rolling volatility / Sharpe / Sortino / beta charts — stats already computed in `q.stats`, just need renderers (quantstats `rolling_volatility`/`rolling_sharpe`/`rolling_sortino`/`rolling_beta` plot wrappers)
  - [ ] Yearly / daily returns bar charts (quantstats `yearly_returns`, `daily_returns`)
  - [x] Monte Carlo fan chart / distribution plot, pairs with `q.stats.montecarlo` (quantstats `plot_montecarlo`, `plot_montecarlo_distribution`)
  - [x] Variance Testing fan chart, pairs with `q.stats.variance_test` — colors simulated forward trade paths by ruined/profitable/neutral outcome, overlaid with the actual historical ("Real") cumulative return path
  - [x] Noise Test fan chart, pairs with `q.stats.noise_test` — colors noise-perturbed return paths by bust/goal/neutral outcome (when given), overlaid with the actual historical ("Real") cumulative return path
- [ ] Interactive plots (plain plotly backend)
- [ ] Animated plots — play button / slider to watch values evolve over time
- [ ] Shared style/config presets (theme, figsize, grids)
- [ ] Tearsheets (performance reports, baked into `q.plot`):
  - [ ] MVP report from a returns series
  - [ ] Sharpe/Sortino/Calmar, drawdowns, rolling stats
  - [ ] Monthly returns heatmap
  - [ ] Benchmark comparison (e.g. vs SPY)
  - [ ] HTML export
  - [ ] Comprehensive metrics table — basic vs. full mode, multi-strategy columns, benchmark column (quantstats `reports.metrics`)
  - [ ] Full 3-panel "snapshot" report: cumulative return / drawdown / daily returns in one figure (quantstats `snapshot`)

## `q.feat` — feature engineering

- [ ] Indicator wrappers: TA-Lib (`q.feat.talib`) and pandas-ta-classic (`q.feat.pandas_ta`)
- [ ] Hand-rolled primitives in `q.feat.qta` (sma, lags, pct_rank, ...)
- [ ] tsfresh wrappers for automated feature extraction
- [ ] Feature pipelines: apply a set of features across a universe of symbols

## `q.model.selection` — leakage-safe data splitting

- [ ] Walk-forward splits (`q.model.selection.timeseries_split`)
- [ ] Purged K-fold with embargo (López de Prado)
- [ ] Combinatorial purged CV
- [ ] Split visualization (plot which samples are train/test/embargo)

## `q.bt` — backtesting

- [ ] Event-driven backtester for model signals
- [ ] Transaction costs / slippage models
- [ ] Trade log output compatible with `q.plot` (tearsheets)

## `q.portfolio` — portfolio analysis

- [ ] Attribution and risk decomposition
- [ ] Exposure and turnover analysis
- [ ] Portfolio optimization (skfolio/Riskfolio-style)

## `q.model` — model utilities

- [x] PyTorch model summaries via torchinfo (`q.model.torch.summary`)
- [ ] PyTorch training loop wrappers
- [ ] Checkpointing and inference helpers
- [ ] Time-series-aware dataloaders

## `q.data` — data access

- [ ] Caching of source downloads
- [x] Prepackaged sample datasets (AAPL, SPY, BTC-USD daily OHLCV, 2000-present) for offline use, refreshed via `make datasets` / `tools/update_datasets.py` (`q.data.datasets`)

## `q.data.sources` — market data sources

- [ ] yfinance source
- [ ] Binance source
- [ ] Each source is a plain module (`read()`, etc.) -- no shared base class, same convention as `q.feat`

## `q.utils`

- [ ] `set_seed` and reproducibility helpers

## Inspiration

Libraries we take inspiration from (and in some cases wrap):

| Library | What we borrow |
|---|---|
| [quantstats](https://github.com/ranaroussi/quantstats) | tearsheets, return-stream metrics |
| [pyfolio-reloaded](https://github.com/stefan-jansen/pyfolio-reloaded) | portfolio/performance analysis |
| [alphalens-reloaded](https://github.com/stefan-jansen/alphalens-reloaded) | alpha-factor evaluation |
| [empyrical-reloaded](https://github.com/stefan-jansen/empyrical-reloaded) | risk/performance statistics |
| [tsfresh](https://github.com/blue-yonder/tsfresh) | automated time-series feature extraction |
| [skfolio](https://github.com/skfolio/skfolio) | sklearn-style portfolio optimization |
| [mlfinlab](https://github.com/hudson-and-thames/mlfinlab) | purged CV, embargo, financial ML (López de Prado) |
| [pandas-ta](https://github.com/twopirllc/pandas-ta) | technical indicators |
| [pandas-ta-classic](https://github.com/xgboosted/pandas-ta-classic) | maintained fork of pandas-ta indicators |
| [Riskfolio-Lib](https://github.com/dcajasn/Riskfolio-Lib) | portfolio optimization & risk measures |
| [qlib](https://github.com/microsoft/qlib) | end-to-end quant ML platform design |
| [pytorch-forecasting](https://github.com/sktime/pytorch-forecasting) | PyTorch time-series model wrappers |
| [sktime](https://github.com/sktime/sktime) | unified time-series API design |
| [alphatools](https://github.com/marketneutral/alphatools) | alpha research & factor tooling on a securities master |
| [PyStats](https://github.com/marcizhu/PyStats) | statistical distribution functions (pdf/cdf/quantile/sampling) |
